mission: Ship more reliable agentic workflows for A3RO; reduce token spend via architecture, not model swaps.

findings: 20 files modified and uncommitted directly on main, no feature branch
Two stale agent branches with zero commits ahead of main: jarvis/fix-lint-fix-4, jarvis/fix-tsc-fix-3
No test framework present anywhere in repo (no jest/vitest/pytest, no *.test.* files)
No CI gate on push/PR; 5 existing GitHub workflows are all scheduled cron jobs that commit data to main without lint/typecheck/test checks
Stack: Next.js 14 / React 18 / TypeScript frontend, Python backend (module5-scan-pipeline), Postgres via pg
Multiple standalone architecture docs exist (bull-market-finder, bagholder-trigger-trade, earnings-beat-tracker) describing multi-module agent/pipeline designs

success_criteria: All changes land via branch + PR, never direct commit to main
Any agent-authored change revertable in under 2 minutes
At least one CI check (lint+typecheck minimum) blocks bad merges
One concrete before/after token-spend measurement tied to a named architectural change
jarvis/* stale branches resolved (merged, cherry-picked, or deleted with reason)

knowledge: Commit as unit of thought vs. unit of agent-turn
revert vs. reset vs. checkout and why revert is safe on shared history
Small diffs as a reliability technique, not a style preference
skills: Triage 20 uncommitted files into atomic commits on a branch
Resolve the two stale jarvis/* branches with documented reasoning
Practice a deliberate git revert to build the reflex
Adopt branch-naming + commit-message convention for agent-authored work
curriculum.wisdom_prompt: What does 'small enough to revert confidently' mean for an A3RO change?

knowledge: Where cost accumulates: context re-injection, tool-result echoing, sub-agent handoff payloads, retries
skills: Instrument one real workflow (Bull Market Finder or Earnings Beat pipeline) to log tokens per step
Build a step -> tokens in/out -> % of total table
Identify largest contributor and hypothesize why
curriculum.wisdom_prompt: Is the biggest cost driver a context problem, a retry problem, or a decomposition problem?

knowledge: Risk-based test targeting for a zero-coverage codebase
Testing agent workflows: determinism, golden-file/snapshot comparisons, smoke tests for unattended cron jobs
skills: Write 3-5 tests around the highest-risk unattended path (scan pipeline or earnings backfill)
Add a CI workflow running lint+typecheck+tests on every push/PR
Intentionally break something small and confirm CI catches it
curriculum.wisdom_prompt: What is your rule of thumb for 'needs a test before merge' vs 'safe to ship directly'?

knowledge: Structural token-reduction patterns: caching context, summarizing instead of re-passing, checkpointed resumable steps
Orchestrator/worker vs. pipeline vs. single-agent-with-tools trade-offs for a multi-tracker system
skills: Apply one architectural change to the Week 2 workflow
Re-measure token spend and record the delta
Write a one-page ADR: problem, alternatives, choice, rationale
curriculum.wisdom_prompt: Are you shipping without fear yet? What's the next cycle's mission?
